recipe.title: Chicken Enchiladas (Tex-Mex style)

ingredients.name: flour tortillas
ingredients.note: recipe default; corn tortillas are an optional variation requiring different handling

ingredients.name: Mexican-blend shredded cheese
ingredients.note: plus extra for topping; source suggests Asadero or Pepper Jack as alternatives

ingredients.name: red enchilada sauce
ingredients.note: homemade preferred per source; linked separate recipe

ingredients.name: optional toppings
ingredients.note: cilantro, chopped red onion, avocado, sour cream, cotija—or quick-pickled red onion suggested in article

steps.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and prepare red enchilada sauce (homemade batch or chosen substitute).
steps.visualCue: oven digital glow, saucepan with dark red sauce
steps.textureCue: sauce surface tension and slow simmer gloss
steps.runwayRisk: oven UI text may need framing that avoids readable labels

steps.text: Heat oil in a large sauté pan over medium-high heat; sauté onion until softened, stirring occasionally (~3 minutes).
steps.textureCue: onion translucency and light edge gold
steps.runwayRisk: stove knob labels; keep macro on pan center

steps.text: Add diced chicken and green chiles; season generously with salt and pepper. Sauté until chicken cooks through (~6–8 minutes), stirring occasionally.
steps.textureCue: steam burst, juicy chicken crumb texture
steps.runwayRisk: undercooked pink may read unappetizing; prefer fully cooked crumb reference

steps.text: Stir in drained beans until evenly mixed; remove pan from heat.
steps.visualCue: dark beans folded through chicken mixture
steps.textureCue: bean skins catching light, creamy-cheesy potential when cheese later added
steps.runwayRisk: bean mash can look muddy; emphasize whole beans

steps.text: Assembly line: lay tortilla, spread ~2 tbsp enchilada sauce on surface, add generous spoonful of filling down the center, sprinkle ~1/3 cup cheese.
steps.visualCue: burrito-style longitudinal fill on flat tortilla
steps.textureCue: sauce drag sheen vs dry tortilla edge
steps.runwayRisk: sauce thickness varies; fragile spread geometry

steps.text: Roll tortilla and place seam logic down in greased 9x13-inch baking dish; repeat for all tortillas.
steps.textureCue: soft tortilla flex and tight cylinder stack
steps.runwayRisk: hand geometry during roll must stay readable without extra fingers

steps.text: Pour remaining sauce evenly over tops of rolls; sprinkle any extra cheese on top.
steps.visualCue: red sauce cascading into gaps between rolls
steps.textureCue: sauce pooling gloss and snowy cheese snowfall
steps.runwayRisk: sauce may hide roll edges; alternating angles help

steps.text: Bake uncovered ~20 minutes until hot through with lightly crisp tortilla exterior; rest briefly then serve hot with toppings.
steps.visualCue: bubbly cheese blotches and sauce steam from dish
steps.textureCue: molten stretch prep, crispy tortilla lip
steps.runwayRisk: cheese burn vs pale; need clear melt reference

assumptions: Flour tortillas are the default styling for Ingredient visuals to match typical Tex-Mex presentation in the article; corn tortillas are noted as variation with flash-fry or dip-in-warm-sauce workaround.

Homemade red enchilada sauce is treated as culturally linked ingredient (linked recipe); we show simmering thickened sauce visuals rather than reciting full sauce sub-steps unless reference images are expanded later.
Bake timing follows source (20 minutes uncovered at 350°F after assembly).
criticalTransformations: Onion sauté to translucent with light gold before proteins added
Chicken dice cooks from pink/raw to opaque with released juices binding to chiles
Thin enchilada sauce spread on tortillas vs thicker batch used for topping—viscosity tells the story
Cheese sprinkled inside roll vs cheese layer on top—two melt behaviors across bake cycle
Bake drives sauce reduction at edges while tortilla rims dry to gentle crisp against molten valleys
visualTextureOpportunities: Simmering red sauce: slow popping bubbles and chili-oil shimmer
Hot oil shimmer right before diced onion rains in
Steam veil lifting off chicken as it sears-white in the sauté pan
Spoon smoothing thick red stripes across pale tortilla—not a flood, an intentional drag
Cheese snowfall on sauce-wet rolls with dusty micro-strands catching light
Post-bake blistered cheese micro-domes and mahogany sauce corners in the casserole
Cold sour cream ribbon over hot surface creating contrast melt-track
Avocado emerald against deep red sauce on the slice or fork bite
possibleHooks: Open on extreme macro bubbles breaking across scarlet simmering sauce with steam curls
Hard cut from sauce macro to audible sizzle as chicken and chiles meet the pan
Late beat: spatula lifting one roll revealing sauce lacquer on the tortilla skirt
promptPolicySources: Workspace rules: seedance-references.mdc (References mode, @KitchenIslandDefault baseline, 9 image cap, ASMR audio only, no speech/music/text)
Workspace rules: recipe-agent-core.mdc (30–48 logical scenes, 5–10 Seedance segments, texture-first opening, end on satisfied character in Licorn kitchen)
Production defaults: style_preset=asmr_food, video_model=seedance2, reference_image_model=gpt_image_2, tts_model=eleven_multilingual_v2, sfx_model=eleven_text_to_sound_v2

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the hero assembly and bake reference flour tortillas (softer, larger rolls) or corn tortillas (smaller, more rustic edges, often pre-softened in oil or sauce)?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Changes roll geometry, surface color, and reference images for fragile rolled states.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Do you want the video to include on-camera roux-based sauce building (oil, flour, spice bloom, stock reduction) or only the finished simmering sauce tied to the enchilada steps?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Determines whether an extra segment and additional state references are needed for flour bloom and whisk tracks.
